code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes and whitespace)
    def _clean(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_clean(o)}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {_clean(o)} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {_clean(o)}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {_clean(o)}" if any(tok in _clean(o).lower() for tok in ["division", "league", "premier", "championship", "liga", "serie"]) else f"{s} plays as a {_clean(o)}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {_clean(o)}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {_clean(o)}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} previously played for {_clean(o)}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {_clean(o)}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {_clean(o)}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {_clean(o)}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {_clean(o)}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft round was {_clean(o)}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {_clean(o)}th pick",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {_clean(o)}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} played youth football for {_clean(o)}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {_clean(o)}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {_clean(o)}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut with {_clean(o)}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {_clean(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {_clean(o)}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {_clean(o)}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {_clean(o)}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {_clean(o)}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the home ground of {s}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an ethnic group of {_clean(o)}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language in {s} is {_clean(o).replace(' language','')}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {_clean(o).replace(' language','')}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {_clean(o)}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {_clean(o)}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {_clean(o)}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {_clean(o)} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build a compact description per entity
    # Also collect manager information for clubs to enable relative clauses
    subject_map = {}
    manager_of = {}  # club -> manager name
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))
        if t.predicate == "manager":
            # store manager for the club (subject)
            manager_of[t.subject] = t.object

    def join_parts(parts):
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last element."""
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        return ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]

    sentences = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        # Preserve the order of appearance for readability
        phrase_parts = []
        # Handle predicates that may have multiple objects (e.g., club, currentclub)
        multi_obj_preds = {"club", "currentclub"}
        # Collect objects for multi‑object predicates first
        multi_objs = {p: [] for p in multi_obj_preds}
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in multi_obj_preds:
                multi_objs[pred].append(obj)
            else:
                # Basic phrase for the predicate
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)

                # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, add a relative clause with its simple descriptors
                if obj in subject_map:
                    # Collect simple descriptors of the linked entity
                    linked_parts = []
                    for p2, o2 in subject_map[obj]:
                        if p2 in {"ground", "stadium", "city", "country"}:
                            linked_parts.append(f"{p2} is {o2}")
                    if linked_parts:
                        phrase += " whose " + ", ".join(linked_parts)

                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Add combined phrases for multi‑object predicates
        for pred, objs in multi_objs.items():
            if objs:
                if len(objs) > 1:
                    objs_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
                else:
                    objs_str = objs[0]
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, objs_str)
                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Join all predicate phrases for this subject into one clause
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""

        return ""

        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o}" if any(tok in o.lower() for tok in ["division", "league", "premier", "championship", "liga", "serie"]) else f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",

        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} played youth football for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut with {o}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{o} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an ethnic group of {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language in {s} is {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build a compact description per entity
    # Also collect manager information for clubs to enable relative clauses
    subject_map = {}
    manager_of = {}  # club -> manager name

        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))
        if t.predicate == "manager":
            # store manager for the club (subject)
            manager_of[t.subject] = t.object

    def join_parts(parts):
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last element."""
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        return ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]

    sentences = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        # Preserve the order of appearance for readability
        phrase_parts = []
        # Handle predicates that may have multiple objects (e.g., club, currentclub)
        multi_obj_preds = {"club", "currentclub"}
        # Collect objects for multi‑object predicates first
        multi_objs = {p: [] for p in multi_obj_preds}
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in multi_obj_preds:
                multi_objs[pred].append(obj)

                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Add combined phrases for multi‑object predicates
        for pred, objs in multi_objs.items():

                    objs_str = objs[0]
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, objs_str)
                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Join all predicate phrases for this subject into one clause
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

        # Collect objects for multi‑object predicates first
        multi_objs = {p: [] for p in multi_obj_preds}
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in multi_obj_preds:
                multi_objs[pred].append(obj)
            else:
                # Basic phrase for the predicate
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)

                # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, add a relative clause with its simple descriptors
                if obj in subject_map:
                    # Collect simple descriptors of the linked entity
                    linked_parts = []
                    for p2, o2 in subject_map[obj]:
                        if p2 in {"ground", "stadium", "city", "country"}:
                            linked_parts.append(f"{p2} is {o2}")
                    if linked_parts:
                        phrase += " whose " + ", ".join(linked_parts)

                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Add combined phrases for multi‑object predicates
